headline: Iran Ceasefire 'On Life Support' as Trump Rejects Tehran Proposal; Ukraine War Resumes After 3-Day Truce; Anthropic Agent 'Dreaming' Goes Live
summary: The US-Iran standoff deepened Tuesday as Trump called Tehran's peace counteroffer 'totally unacceptable' and Iran vowed to 'never bow,' leaving the Strait of Hormuz sealed for a tenth consecutive week with Saudi Aramco warning no resolution may come in 2026. The US-brokered Russia-Ukraine 3-day ceasefire (May 9–11) expired with 200+ clashes logged and both sides resuming operations, while Trump's Beijing summit with Xi Jinping remains on track for May 14–15. In AI, Anthropic's new 'dreaming' self-improvement feature for Claude Managed Agents is drawing enterprise attention after Harvey reported a 6× task-completion lift, and the Senate is expected to vote this week on Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh as Powell's term expires May 15.

highlights.title: Iran Rejects US Peace Proposal — Trump Calls Response 'Totally Unacceptable,' Hormuz Closed 10 Weeks
highlights.summary: Iran sent its formal counterproposal to the US 14-point MOU via Pakistan as mediator; Trump immediately rejected it as 'totally unacceptable.' Tehran demanded war reparations, full Hormuz sovereignty, full sanctions removal, and release of frozen assets — well beyond the US framework. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued 'new and decisive military directives' without elaboration. The Strait of Hormuz has now been closed for ten consecutive weeks, with Saudi Aramco publicly warning the disruption may not be resolved in 2026. Global food prices rose for a third consecutive month (UN FAO), tied to energy and fertilizer supply shocks from the conflict.

highlights.title: Anthropic 'Dreaming' Feature Lets Claude Agents Self-Improve Overnight — Harvey Reports 6× Lift
highlights.summary: Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents platform now supports 'dreaming': a scheduled asynchronous workflow that reviews prior session transcripts, merges duplicate memories, removes stale entries, and surfaces recurring patterns — all without modifying model weights. Legal AI firm Harvey reported a ~6× increase in task-completion rates after adopting the feature. Two previously experimental features — Outcomes (goal tracking) and Multiagent Orchestration (parallel specialist sub-agent delegation) — also moved from research preview to public beta. This represents a meaningful step toward persistent, self-improving enterprise AI agents that compound performance over time.

highlights.title: Kevin Warsh Senate Floor Vote Imminent as Powell's Fed Term Expires May 15
highlights.summary: The Senate Banking Committee advanced Trump's Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh 13–11 in the first fully partisan committee vote on a Fed chair in Senate history. A full Senate floor vote is expected this week, potentially confirming Warsh before Powell's term ends May 15. Warsh inherits a Fed that held rates at 3.5–3.75% at its April 29 meeting amid persistent Middle East-linked inflation and four dissenting FOMC votes — the most since 1992. Powell will remain as a Fed governor until 2028, providing some continuity.

perspectives.title: Unacceptable vs. Never Bow: Who Blinks First in the US-Iran Standoff?
perspectives.subtitle: Washington and Tehran frame the same negotiations very differently

sources.name: CNBC / White House framing
sources.quote: Trump called Iran's proposal 'totally unacceptable' — Tehran demanded war reparations, full Hormuz sovereignty, and complete sanctions removal, none of which appeared in the US 14-point framework.

sources.name: Al Jazeera / Iranian framing
sources.quote: Iran says it will 'never bow' to US demands, insisting any agreement must first address the lifting of the naval blockade before nuclear concessions can be discussed — the reverse of the US sequencing.

perspectives.title: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit: Structural Deal or Managed Optics?
perspectives.subtitle: Analysts split on whether Beijing's accumulated leverage changes the outcome

sources.name: CFR / Brookings
sources.quote: China has spent six years accumulating structural leverage — rare-earth dominance, Hormuz-linked oil exposure, and an elevated Taiwan agenda. At this summit, Beijing holds the upper hand and faces little pressure to offer lasting concessions.

sources.name: WEF / CSIS
sources.quote: The summit will likely yield modest but real deliverables — soybean commitments, a Boeing purchase, and a Board of Trade framework — that create stability without resolving the deeper tech and security tensions.

quotes.text: Iran's response to our peace proposal was totally unacceptable. The ceasefire is on massive life support.
Russia's war on Ukraine may be coming to an end. I am willing to meet Zelenskyy in a third country if a peace deal is finalized.

map_hotspots.title: Strait of Hormuz — Closed 10 Weeks
map_hotspots.summary: Iran war has sealed the Strait since late February. Saudi Aramco warns disruption may not resolve in 2026. Global energy supply shock driving food price inflation for third consecutive month.

map_hotspots.title: Ukraine — War Resumes After Ceasefire Expires
map_hotspots.summary: US-brokered 3-day ceasefire (May 9–11) expired with 200+ clashes logged. Both sides blame each other for violations; no extension announced. Putin expressed willingness to meet Zelenskyy in a third country.

map_hotspots.title: Beijing — Trump-Xi Summit in 2 Days
map_hotspots.summary: Trump visits May 14–15. Expected agenda: Board of Trade managed-trade framework, Boeing 500-aircraft deal, soybean commitments, chip export control adjustments, Iran war, Taiwan. CFR warns China holds structural upper hand.

map_hotspots.title: Beirut — Hezbollah Resumes Cross-Border Strikes
map_hotspots.summary: Hezbollah claimed 26 attacks on May 9, including the first strike inside Israel since the April 16 ceasefire, as Iran peace talks stall. Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs May 7.

map_hotspots.title: Tehran — New Supreme Leader Issues Military Directives
map_hotspots.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei issued 'new and decisive military directives' as Iran rejected US peace proposal. Iran parliamentary speaker warns US will be 'surprised' by any military response.

event_updates.title: US-Iran War Ceasefire Negotiations
event_updates.latest_summary: Trump rejected Iran's formal counterproposal as 'totally unacceptable' on May 11. Tehran demanded war reparations, full Hormuz sovereignty, complete sanctions removal, and frozen asset release. Iran vowed to 'never bow.' New Supreme Leader Khamenei issued undisclosed military directives. Strait of Hormuz closed 10 consecutive weeks; Saudi Aramco warns no resolution may come in 2026. The standoff directly clouds the Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 14–15), where Iran is on the agenda.

event_updates.title: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire Negotiations
event_updates.latest_summary: Trump's 3-day May 9–11 ceasefire expired with combat resuming immediately. Ukraine logged 200+ Russian violations during the truce. The 1,000-prisoner swap reportedly proceeded. The Kremlin explicitly ruled out any extension. Putin expressed willingness to meet Zelenskyy in a third country contingent on a finalized peace framework — but no framework is currently on the table. Both sides returned to full operational tempo.

event_updates.title: US-China Trade & Chip Export Controls
event_updates.latest_summary: Trump-Xi Beijing summit is now T-2 days (May 14–15). Pre-summit negotiations in Seoul sought to lock in quick deliverables. Expected outcomes: Board of Trade managed-trade framework, Chinese purchase of ~500 Boeing aircraft, sustained US agricultural commodity imports, possible easing on 14nm/7nm chip-equipment export controls. The US simultaneously sanctioned 12 companies facilitating Iran-China oil trade — adding friction to the summit agenda. CFR and Brookings warn China holds structural upper hand.

event_updates.title: AI Regulation & Governance
event_updates.latest_summary: Kevin Warsh Senate floor vote expected this week; confirmation before Powell's May 15 departure would install Trump's preferred Fed chair. EU AI Act enforcement window is now ~90 days to Q3 2026 compliance deadline; several US AI firms may delay European agent product launches. CAISI pre-release model evaluation agreements now cover all five major US labs. Anthropic released Claude Managed Agents 'dreaming' feature with automatic cybersecurity safeguards in Claude Opus 4.7 blocking prohibited use cases.

event_updates.title: AI Agent Ecosystem
event_updates.latest_summary: Anthropic's 'dreaming' feature for Claude Managed Agents moved to public availability, enabling scheduled self-improvement without weight modification. Harvey (legal AI) reports 6× task-completion improvement. Multiagent Orchestration and Outcomes also now in public beta. GPT-5.5 Instant became the default ChatGPT model with Gmail-linked personalization and 52.5% fewer hallucinations. Grok 4.3 and Gemini 3.1 Ultra launched this week, intensifying the frontier model competition.
